CisternaMiddleware(Middleware) async on_call_tool handler for FastMCP v3.
traced_tool double-decorator for FastMCP v2 sync tools.
Per-consumer surface selection with v3 middleware capability probe.
AC-MCP-1..4 test coverage for v3 middleware, v2 decorator, error handling, token safety.
AC-NAMEFREEZE-1..4 AST lint and runtime guard tests for event-name freeze.

verification.acceptance_criteria: AC-MCP-1, AC-MCP-2, AC-MCP-3, AC-MCP-3b, AC-MCP-4, AC-NAMEFREEZE-1, AC-NAMEFREEZE-2, AC-NAMEFREEZE-3, AC-NAMEFREEZE-4

notes: Full M1-MCP layer implemented per spec. Ready for M1-SHADOW parity tests and M1-PERF benchmarks.

findings.issue: AC-MCP-4 cross-context ValueError path tested via source inspection rather than actual injection; the genuine ValueError from reset() is not triggered at runtime
findings.why_relevant: Code structure is verified but the actual runtime exception path is not exercised
New adapter class for Typer CLI with ALLOWED_NAMES={cli.cmd_start,cli.cmd_end}
Decorator for CLI timing; emits events and re-raises exceptions
3 test cases verifying cli.cmd_start/cmd_end emission, custom naming, exception re-raise

test_results: 46 passed (3 new CLI tests + 43 existing regression tests)
acceptance_criteria: AC-CLI-1: CliAdapter with ALLOWED_NAMES; timed_command decorator emits cli.cmd_start/cli.cmd_end; re-raises exceptions
summary: M1-CLI implementation complete. Created src/cisterna/adapters/cli.py with CliAdapter and timed_command decorator per spec §4.2. Decorator emits cli.cmd_start before execution and cli.cmd_end after, recording duration_ms and ok/exc_type fields. On exception, emits cli.cmd_end with ok=False and re-raises (CLI owns exit code). Added tests/test_cli.py with 3 AC-CLI-1 test cases. All tests pass, linting clean.

findings.why_relevant: Does not validate cli.py emit_event names against CliAdapter.ALLOWED_NAMES — spec §4.3 says lint covers all adapter files

summary: EC-3 warn-and-continue policy correctly implemented and tested. All 47 tests pass; ruff clean. One transient test isolation failure when running a subset with residual daemon threads is pre-existing environmental flakiness, not introduced by this commit.

findings.why_relevant: Spec check 1: present; checks p.is_alive()==False AND staleness > 2x interval
Spec check 2: throttles via _last_ec3_warn (60s window)
Spec check 3: contains both EC-3 and pipeline consumer dead
Spec check 4: called at end of _probe_jsonl_file
Spec check 5: present; asserts stderr content

findings.issue: M1-SHADOW commit c5f8ef9 exists on worktree-wf_4e6800fd-064-3 but was not merged into main. git log 831779091b..HEAD on main is empty.
findings.fix: Merge worktree branch worktree-wf_4e6800fd-064-3 into main.

findings.issue: duration_tolerance_ms parameter declared but never used in function body. Docstring promises timestamp tolerance checking that isn't implemented.
findings.fix: Either remove the unused parameter or implement the timestamp comparison logic.

findings.issue: M1-PERF commits (418fb45, c5f8ef9) not merged into main. git log 831779091b76df7779d9bfe36a8dd7538b105ab9..HEAD is empty. Commits exist only in worktree branch worktree-wf_4e6800fd-064-7.
findings.fix: Merge worktree branch into main before review.

findings.issue: cleanup fixture does not reset so_mod._last_ec3_warn (spec requirement: 'cleanup fixture resets global pipeline + self_obs state (including _last_ec3_warn)'). Only _heartbeat_thread, _last_stat, _jsonl_path are reset.
findings.fix: Add `so_mod._last_ec3_warn = 0.0` to the cleanup fixture after line 42.

notes: In worktree wf_4e6800fd-064-7: all 3 perf tests pass, full 53-test suite green, ruff clean. Implementation is functionally correct except for the _last_ec3_warn omission in cleanup. Merge is the blocking issue.

findings.issue: main HEAD == base SHA; git log 831779..HEAD empty. M1-PERF commits stranded in unmerged worktree wf_4e6800fd-064-7. test_perf.py absent on main.
AC-SHADOW-1/2 shadow parity harness (capture_legacy/capture_cisterna/assert_parity) NOT implemented anywhere — symbols exist only in spec/plan markdown. No shadow test file.
findings.fix: Implement spec §6 shadow harness + tests/shadow/{bathos,contemplex} gating AC-SHADOW-1,2.

findings.issue: cleanup fixture omits so_mod._last_ec3_warn reset (spec-required); global stateful guard leaks across tests.

notes: Re-confirms prior FAIL (260616_cisterna-m1-sprint3_perf_review). New finding: AC-SHADOW harness entirely unimplemented in both main and the worktree — AC-PERF being stranded is not the only blocker. AC-PERF tests in worktree are high quality and pass there per prior record.

findings.summary: Document is a brainstorm artifact, not an implementation spec: Acceptance Criteria 'Then' is an empty placeholder (line 125), no gates, no DAG, no TBD register. Prompt-referenced AC-ASYNC-1/AC-VALIDATE-3/AC-CLEAR-1/TBD-M2-4/M2-TELEM/backlog-DAG do not exist anywhere in repo.

Pure-metadata marker testability: spec never defines what calling a @cisterna.tool fn returns pre-wire. Decision log line 89 (return-through wrapper) contradicts line 70 (inert stub). Unresolved contradiction.
_make_mcp_callable signature undefined; no handling of pre-wrapped fns (lru_cache/staticmethod). __signature__ injection (H1) interaction with already-wrapped callables unspecified.

E1 shim cannot reach await from a sync _make_mcp_callable. Spec conflates 'generated callable is async' (E2/H2, rejected/deferred) with 'shim detects loop' (E1, accepted). The accepted set is internally inconsistent for the async-MCP path.
M2 Cyclopts scope unresolved (negotiable line 23/122). wire(app=...) behavior undefined if CLI deferred. F1 dual error contract and CLI exit-code handling have no home if CLI out of scope.

Double-instrumentation: M1 CisternaMiddleware (v3_middleware.py:20) already instantiates BathosAdapter and calls emit_start/end/error + shape_ok/error for every tool. M2 wire-time generated callable adding the same telemetry double-counts events and double-shapes returns. No composition contract with active M1 middleware.

clear_registry vs already-wired servers: spec (premortem #3) wants clear_registry for test teardown but never states whether clearing the registry affects servers already wired in a prior test (wire snapshots vs live-proxy B2 unresolved). Test-isolation semantics undefined.

summary: Defended 5 anticipated challenger objections against M2 registration-surface spec. 4 of 5 rebutted with evidence from spec Decision Log + M1 code (v3_middleware.py, cli.py, base.py). Conceded TBD-M2-4 scope ambiguity: the spec's TBDs and ACs 'Then' sections are empty placeholders, so AC-WIRE-2/3 and the M3-CLI backlog placeholder referenced in the defense do not actually exist in the spec text yet.

findings.why_relevant: TBDs section is empty; TBD-M2-4 (CLI scope) is not recorded, so concession is on the spec lacking a recorded decision, not on a wrong one
Then section is a placeholder ('add specific measurable criteria'); AC-WIRE-2/3, AC-TELEM-1 referenced by defense do not yet exist as written ACs
Server-level telemetry+shaping interception point; grounds F1 dual-error-contract rebuttal that M2 compose must not double-wrap MCP
Middleware runs inside async dispatch loop; grounds E1 async-shim rebuttal
CLI re-raises exceptions (active handler); grounds F1 CLI half of dual error contract

summary: All five spec checks satisfied: package scaffold present, CisternaWireError fully implemented, import clean, stubs raise only on call, ruff and pytest both green.

findings.why_relevant: TBD-M2-1 gap: asserts cli_commands populated but not mcp_tools in same dual-transport call
Missing AC-M2-11 and AC-M2-6 from the stated coverage list — traceability gap
Added TBD-M2-1 dual-transport assertions: result.mcp_tools and FastMCP server both contain cli_registered_tool in the same wire(server, app) call
Added AC-M2-11 and AC-M2-6/C5 to coverage list for traceability
Stderr assertion only checks non-empty; does not verify format 'Error (Type): msg'
import time repeated 5x inside test bodies; could be top-level

findings.issue: test_mcp_callable_propagates_exception wires exploding_tool onto server then discards it and composes a fresh callable (also_explodes) to test propagation. The test doesn't exercise the wired path end-to-end.
findings.fix: Either remove the wire(server) setup lines and test compose_mcp_callable directly, or retrieve the callable from the server's internal map and invoke it.

findings.issue: CLI error handler catches BaseException rather than Exception. This catches KeyboardInterrupt and similar signals, converting them into sys.exit(1) rather than letting them propagate.
findings.fix: Use `except Exception as exc` unless signal-hardening is explicitly desired; document the intent if BaseException is intentional.

findings.issue: The 'adapter' parameter is silently accepted and never validated; a caller passing an adapter with a typo in the keyword gets no feedback.
findings.fix: Low priority — current behaviour is intentional for forward-compat. Could add a debug-level log when adapter is non-None to aid developers.

checks: single SpyAdapter in conftest.py; no duplicate definitions in wire/telem test files
test_mcp_callable_propagates_exception uses wired callable; stderr test pins format; import time module-level
AC-M2-5b uses pydantic.Field with exact description assertions
C5/AC-M2-6 intact - zero adapter method calls in wired.py/compose.py production code

summary: M2 registration surface is structurally sound and spec-adherent. All 14 ACs are satisfied. C5 invariant is clean: compose.py and wired.py contain zero adapter/telemetry references. Two minor-severity items noted for follow-up: (1) AC-M2-5b pydantic.Field description assertion is an improvement over bare strings but still a runtime FastMCP schema check that could silently pass on schema shape alone; (2) CLI callable in wired.py has no __signature__ set, so Cyclopts argument parsing relies on the original fn's annotations being discoverable through the __doc__/__name__ copy — not a correctness issue today but a latent Cyclopts introspection brittleness.

findings.issue: CLI callable (_cli_cmd) copies __name__ and __doc__ from original fn but does NOT copy __signature__ or __annotations__. Cyclopts uses inspect.signature() for argument parsing. Today it works because Cyclopts walks __wrapped__ or __annotations__ of the registered function, but if that heuristic changes, CLI argument parsing silently breaks.

AC-M2-5b asserts description appears in FastMCP schema properties. This is correct and much stronger than the prior bare-string approach. However, the test verifies type AND description equality per field but does not assert 'required' or 'default' propagation for the 'times' param (which has a default). Not a blocker, just a coverage gap.
findings.fix: Optional: add assert 'times' not in tool.parameters.get('required', []) to confirm default propagation into schema.

findings.issue: test_cli_callable_writes_message_to_stderr has a bare except Exception: pass fallback on line 371 that could silently swallow unexpected exceptions from app() before stderr is checked. If cyclopts raises something other than SystemExit, the test may pass trivially (empty stderr, assertion fails). Low risk because the actual test assertion fires after.
findings.fix: Replace bare except with: except SystemExit: pass — then let other exceptions propagate so failures are surfaced.

findings.issue: clear_registry(name=None) treats None as 'default'. The pre-mortem record in the spec (line 125) calls it clear_registry(registry=None) — the parameter was renamed to 'name' in implementation. This is a non-breaking rename but the pre-mortem doc is slightly inconsistent with the actual API.
findings.fix: Documentation only: update internal pre-mortem note if the spec is ever republished; no code change needed.

previously_resolved_confirmed: BaseException->Exception narrowing in wired.py CLI handler: CONFIRMED resolved (wired.py:181 — except Exception as exc)
SpyAdapter/_MinimalAdapter consolidated into conftest.py: CONFIRMED resolved
registry.py documented as not-thread-safe: CONFIRMED resolved (registry.py:12-13)

summary: F1/F2/F3 addressed, 155 tests green, ruff clean, C5 invariant intact.

Challenged M3 buildable spec against real code (registry.py, decorator.py, __init__.py, adapters/cli.py) and research note. Found 3 BLOCKER + 6 MAJOR within the locked shape. Registry mapping accurate but iteration order, plugin.json shape vs Claude convention, provenance two-pass on JSON-vs-md, --import side-effect/registry-isolation, and N8 DAG dependency are the load-bearing gaps.

findings.issue: plugin.json commands as bare names contradicts Claude convention requiring commands/<name>.md frontmatter; spec never specifies the .md frontmatter that Claude actually loads.

plugin.json (JSON) excluded from stamping but INCLUDED in bundle_sha256 input — re-running is idempotent only if stamp-append never alters which files are .md; spec does not state plugin.json is hashed-but-not-stamped explicitly, and the stamp digest covers plugin.json too, so editing any .md changes nothing in plugin.json but the digest stamped into .md files is over the WHOLE set incl plugin.json. Ordering OK but under-specified.

importlib.import_module is cached in sys.modules; a second export run in same process re-imports nothing (decorator side-effects don't re-fire), and pytest tests sharing process see registry contamination — no registry-clean fixture in conftest.py (only per-file).

summary: M3 buildable spec is ~90% implementable as written. Locked decisions + 5 premortems + ToolEntry shape verified in code resolve the bulk of likely objections (never-raise mapping, pure-emit ABC, two-pass hashing, --import mechanism, DAG, public API). Genuine gaps conceded: (G1) DAG node N2 file path 'assets/source.py' contradicts §1 which places the source function in assets/ but never names source.py vs spec.py — minor but unstated; (G2) CommandAsset.body provenance: spec says commands/<n>.md = 'body + provenance stamp' but registry-sourced CommandAssets have body='' so the .md emits description only — emit must define what goes in the .md body when body is empty; (G3) plugin.json description when meta.description default is '' — emitted as empty string, faithful but version source for BundleMetadata.version is unspecified (CLI must supply it).

findings.why_relevant: G2: .md body content undefined when registry-sourced body is empty
G1: source module filename never named in §1 data model section
G3: version field has no default and no specified source in CLI build path

issues.reason: rev2 reconciled all FATAL/MAJOR challenger findings

summary: TIER3 challenger/defender review of M3.1b scope. 3 FATALs reconciled (copilot hook surfaces L15, export --surface L20, golden fixture table). MAJORs resolved in rev3. OPTION-C Antigravity phasing locked L21.
